title: Monotone-constrained booster on compressed physical pressure features
summary: Boosted trees can enforce monotonicity when domain knowledge is strong.
rationale: Boosted trees can enforce monotonicity when domain knowledge is strong. Here, several engineered scalars already summarize physics cleanly: service-window pressure, finishability pressure, laps-since-pit, and tyre-age overage. Constraining only those compressed pressures may reduce shift-driven overfitting without over-constraining raw inputs.
implementation_hint: Create a small feature subset of interpretable pressures/ratios, then train XGBoost/LightGBM/CatBoost with positive monotone constraints on risk-like features and negative constraints on safety-margin features. Keep this as a conservative specialist and blend by rank/probability with an unconstrained model.
expected_effect: Usually modest CV gain at best, but can improve public-score stability by preventing pathological non-physical splits.
risk: Wrong constraint direction on raw features will hurt; constraints should be applied only to already-sanitized pressure features, not broad raw columns.
